edges.description: Expanding ESCC tumors acquire PD-L1-linked checkpoint programs that enable immune escape.
Tumor growth progressively narrows the esophageal lumen and impairs swallowing.
Tumor burden and impaired oral intake contribute to weight loss.
Columnar (Barrett) metaplasia of the distal esophageal mucosa is the obligate histologic precursor of EAC.
Invasive EAC subsets acquire PD-L1-linked checkpoint programs that enable immune escape.
Tumor growth progressively narrows the esophageal lumen and impairs swallowing.
Tumor burden and impaired oral intake contribute to weight loss.

nodes.description: A clinically meaningful subset of advanced esophageal carcinoma (both ESCC and EAC, including Barrett-associated dysplastic and invasive lesions) expresses PD-L1 on tumor cells and tumor-infiltrating immune cells, consistent with a checkpoint-mediated adaptive immune-resistance state targetable with PD-1 blockade.

biological_processes: negative regulation of T cell mediated immunity

evidence.reference_title: PD-L1 expression and its clinicopathological correlation in advanced esophageal squamous cell carcinoma in a Chinese population.
evidence.snippet: PD-L1 was expressed on 29.9% (113/378) ESCC tumor cells and 40.2% (152/378) tumor-infiltrating immune cells.

evidence.reference_title: PD-L1 expression in gastroesophageal dysplastic lesions.
evidence.snippet: A higher prevalence of PD-L1-positive cases was observed among esophageal specimens compared with gastric ones (p = 0.0003), in high-grade and adenocarcinoma samples in comparison with low-grade dysplasia (p < 0.0001), and in lesions with mismatch repair deficiency (p = 0.028).

nodes.description: In patients with resected esophageal or gastroesophageal junction cancer who have residual pathologic disease after neoadjuvant chemoradiotherapy, adjuvant nivolumab significantly prolongs disease-free survival.

evidence.reference_title: Adjuvant Nivolumab in Resected Esophageal or Gastroesophageal Junction Cancer.
evidence.snippet: Among patients with resected esophageal or gastroesophageal junction cancer who had received neoadjuvant chemoradiotherapy, disease-free survival was significantly longer among those who received nivolumab adjuvant therapy than among those who received placebo.

evidence.reference_title: Screening for Barrett Esophagus and Esophageal Adenocarcinoma: Approaches and Outcomes.
evidence.snippet: Barrett esophagus (BE) is the only known histological precursor to esophageal adenocarcinoma (EAC).

nodes.description: In esophageal adenocarcinoma, chronic gastroesophageal reflux drives columnar (Barrett) metaplasia of the distal esophageal mucosa, which progresses through dysplasia to invasive adenocarcinoma. The transition is accompanied by progressive TP53 inactivation and accumulating chromosomal instability with recurrent copy-number changes including ERBB2 amplification.

evidence.reference_title: Screening for Barrett Esophagus and Esophageal Adenocarcinoma: Approaches and Outcomes.
evidence.snippet: Barrett esophagus (BE) is the only known histological precursor to esophageal adenocarcinoma (EAC).

evidence.reference_title: Genomic and Transcriptomic Characteristics of Esophageal Adenocarcinoma.
evidence.snippet: The tumor suppressor gene TP53 is mutated in 70 to 80% of tumors followed by genomic alterations in CDKN2A, KRAS, ERBB2, ARID1A, SMAD4 and a long tail of less frequently mutated genes.

nodes.description: Progressive difficulty swallowing solids, then liquids, is the most common presenting symptom of esophageal carcinoma due to luminal narrowing by tumor.

In esophageal squamous cell carcinoma, chronic exposure of stratified squamous epithelium to tobacco-derived carcinogens and acetaldehyde from alcohol metabolism produces DNA damage and mutational burden that selects for TP53 loss-of-function and recurrent alterations in cell-cycle, squamous differentiation, and NOTCH signaling.

evidence.reference_title: Alcohol drinking, cigarette smoking, and the development of squamous cell carcinoma of the esophagus: molecular mechanisms of carcinogenesis.
evidence.snippet: Both alcohol consumption and cigarette smoking are major risk factors for the development of ESCC.

evidence.reference_title: Integrated genomic characterization of oesophageal carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: Squamous cell carcinomas showed frequent genomic amplifications of CCND1 and SOX2 and/or TP63, whereas ERBB2, VEGFA and GATA4 and GATA6 were more commonly amplified in adenocarcinomas.

nodes.description: Adding the anti-PD-1 antibody pembrolizumab to first-line fluoropyrimidine-platinum chemotherapy improves overall and progression-free survival in advanced esophageal carcinoma, with the largest benefit in ESCC and in PD-L1 CPS-high disease.

evidence.reference_title: Pembrolizumab plus chemotherapy versus chemotherapy alone for first-line treatment of advanced oesophageal cancer (KEYNOTE-590): a randomised, placebo-controlled, phase 3 study.
evidence.snippet:
Compared with placebo plus chemotherapy, pembrolizumab plus 
chemotherapy improved overall survival in patients with previously untreated, 
advanced oesophageal squamous cell carcinoma and PD-L1 CPS of 10 or more, and 
overall survival and progression-free survival in patients with oesophageal 
squamous cell carcinoma, PD-L1 CPS of 10 or more, and in all randomised patients 
regardless of histology

nodes.description: Unintentional weight loss is a frequent presenting feature, reflecting both impaired oral intake from dysphagia and cancer cachexia.
